mission.title: Install and Configure Charmed Kubernetes
mission.description: Charmed Kubernetes is a distribution of Kubernetes that utilizes Juju for deployment and management, providing a flexible and scalable solution for running Kubernetes clusters. It is particularly suited for users who prefer a model-driven approach to managing Kubernetes and want to leverage the power of Ubuntu. Key trade-offs include the need for Juju knowledge and potential complexity in initial setup compared to simpler installation methods.

steps.title: Install Juju
steps.description:
Juju is required to deploy Charmed Kubernetes. This command installs Juju on your system.

```bash

sudo snap install juju --classic

```

> This installs the latest stable version of Juju.

steps.title: Bootstrap the Juju controller
steps.description:
This command initializes the Juju controller, which manages your Kubernetes deployment.

```bash

juju bootstrap localhost

```

> This command sets up a local Juju controller.

steps.title: Deploy Charmed Kubernetes
steps.description:
This command deploys the Charmed Kubernetes bundle, which includes all necessary components.

```bash

juju deploy charmed-kubernetes

```

> This deploys the latest stable version of Charmed Kubernetes.

steps.title: Verify the deployment
steps.description:
This command checks the status of the deployed services to ensure everything is running correctly.

```bash

juju status

```

> Look for all services to be in the 'active' state.

steps.title: Configure Kubernetes access
steps.description:
This command sets up your kubeconfig file to access the Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

juju config kubernetes-master kubeconfig

```

> This retrieves the kubeconfig for your cluster.

uninstall.title: Remove Charmed Kubernetes
uninstall.description:
This command removes the Charmed Kubernetes deployment and all associated resources. Be aware that this will result in data loss.

```bash

juju destroy-controller localhost --destroy-all-models

```

> This command will delete the controller and all models.

uninstall.title: Clean up orphaned resources
uninstall.description:
After removing the deployment, ensure to check for any orphaned resources that may incur costs.

```bash

juju remove-application charmed-kubernetes

```

> This ensures all applications are removed.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Charmed Kubernetes
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, back up your data and ensure your cluster is healthy. This process will upgrade your Charmed Kubernetes deployment.

```bash

juju deploy charmed-kubernetes --revision <new-revision>

juju status

```

> Replace <new-revision> with the desired version.
> Verify that all services are running after the upgrade.

upgrade.title: Backup your data
upgrade.description:
Always back up your data before performing an upgrade.

```bash

juju run --all 'kubectl get all --all-namespaces -o yaml > backup.yaml'

```

> This command backs up all resources to a YAML file.
commands: juju run --all 'kubectl get all --all-namespaces -o yaml > backup.yaml'

upgrade.title: Post-upgrade verification
upgrade.description:
After the upgrade, verify that all components are functioning correctly.

```bash

juju status

```

> Check that all services are active and healthy.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'juju bootstrap failed'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur if the Juju controller cannot connect to the cloud provider.

**Fix:**
Check your cloud credentials and network connectivity.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'kubernetes-master not responding'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the Kubernetes master is not reachable or not running.

**Fix:**
Run 'juju status' to check the status of the services and troubleshoot accordingly.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'juju deploy failed'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may happen if the bundle is not available or there are network issues.

**Fix:**
Ensure that you have access to the internet and the bundle is correctly specified.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'kubeconfig not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the kubeconfig file has not been generated or is missing.

**Fix:**
Run 'juju config kubernetes-master kubeconfig' to generate the kubeconfig.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for Kubernetes v1.31 features, including new resource quotas and improved scheduling.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated support for older Kubernetes versions below 1.29.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from v1.30, ensure to review the new resource quota configurations.

resolution.summary: You should see output from 'juju status' indicating all services are active and healthy, with no errors reported.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a compatible environment with sufficient resources and the Juju tool installed.
